summary.title: Lexical port bundle: 7 rules to Lexicon signature; sprawl/imposters _v2 cleanup
summary.what: Ports six lexical rules (cowards, tautology, hammers, slackers, confusion, stutter) from the legacy (root, rc, sc) signature to (lexicon, rc, sc). Drops the legacy_v2_shim wrapper from all seven shimmed lexical rules — zero shim references remain across the structural+lexical registries. Adds Lexicon.callables() and Lexicon.files() view methods. Renames sprawl/imposters _v2 functions to canonical names and deletes their legacy path-based duplicates. Deletes 4 retired _lexical/ kernels (cowards, tautology, hammers, slackers). Adds _derive_root helper to rules that still need root resolution (prefers explicit slop_config.root, falls back to lexicon parses' common path). Full suite 522 passed + 8 xfailed.
summary.why: Push the lexical rules onto the substrate signature to set up the 'invention' phase the user signalled comes after the port. With shims gone and the rule-layer surface uniform, the remaining work is migrating compute (FCA in sprawl, cluster body-profile in imposters, scope-chain walk in stutter, file-level clustering in confusion) onto Lexicon view methods. That's where the slop research re-opens.

decisions.chose: Inline cowards/tautology/hammers logic into rule files; retire those kernels outright
decisions.rationale: These three rules' computation is self-contained (suffix pattern matching, type-tag tables, banlist scanning). The kernels added wrapper layers over what is fundamentally one classifier + one iterator. Inlining the classifier into the rule and reading entities from Lexicon retires the kernel cleanly.

decisions.chose: Keep slackers/confusion/stutter calling their kernels (signature-only port)
decisions.rationale: These rules ride deeper compute — slackers needs the imposters body-profile pass (receiver-call density, body Jaccard, modal overlap); confusion needs imposters' file-scoped clustering; stutter walks scope chains across the whole AST. Implementing those as view methods is substantive substrate work that warrants its own intents (the 'invention' phase). For now the rule signature is uniform, the shim is gone, and the kernel call is the implementation detail.

decisions.chose: _derive_root helper: prefer explicit slop_config.root over lexicon parses
decisions.rationale: Tests scope a Lexicon to tmp_path and pass slop_config(root=tmp_path); legacy rules took root as the first positional arg. The new helper checks slop_config.root first (treats default '.' as not-set) so package-scope detection works when the Lexicon's common path is deeper than the search root. Fall-back to lexicon parses common path handles tests that omit root entirely.

decisions.chose: Rename sprawl/imposters _v2 → canonical instead of leaving suffix
decisions.rationale: Matches the structural-suite naming-spec cleanup. _v2 was migration scaffolding; once the legacy path-based versions are gone there's only one implementation. Identifier names should describe what the function does, not which port iteration produced it.

review_notes: Zero legacy_v2_shim references remain in slop/lexicon/rules/__init__.py and slop/structure/rules/__init__.py. The shim machinery in slop.linter._shim still exists (no current consumers) and can retire when convenient.
Six lexical kernels remain in _lexical/: confusion, imposters, sprawl, stutter, _naming, _words. The first four are load-bearing for their respective rules; _naming and _words support those kernels.
CLI smoke on slop's src/: 1 violation + 1524 advisories across 9 lexical rules. Substrate signature works end-to-end.

_derive_root choosing slop_config.root over lexicon parses is the key behavioural fix that unblocked the package-scope tests in test_sprawl_imposters.py — without it, the lexicon's common path was deeper than the user-intended search root, hiding multi-file clusters.

architectural_claims: Lexical rule suite is uniformly substrate-signature: every run_X(lexicon, rc, sc). No legacy_v2_shim wrappers remain in either rule registry.
Lexicon gains callables() and files() iterators for rules needing parameter access or per-file iteration.
Three rules (cowards, tautology, hammers) are now fully substrate-native; their pattern-matching logic lives in the rule file and consumes lexicon iterators.
behavioral_changes: Six rule signatures change from (root, rc, sc) to (lexicon, rc, sc): cowards, tautology, hammers, slackers, confusion, stutter.
Two rule functions rename (drop _v2 suffix): run_sprawl_v2 → run_sprawl, run_imposters_v2 → run_imposters.
Test files migrated to pass Lexicon (via _lexicon helper) instead of Path.
Hammers rule summary key 'files_searched' is removed (no longer relevant).
migration_notes: Custom code importing run_*_v2 functions from slop.lexicon.rules.{sprawl,imposters} should drop the _v2 suffix.

Custom code importing cowards_kernel / tautology_kernel / hammers_kernel / slackers_kernel will break. Use Tree.lexicon's named_entities() / callables() / files() and reuse the inlined classifier logic (now lives in the rule file) if needed externally.
